steps.title: Read the context
steps.content: Marisol starts with $160$ and earns $3\%$ more each year. One year later the amount is $160(1.03)=164.80$. Use the values at $x=0$ and $x=1$ directly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Decreasing exponential: The amount grows each year (3% more), so it is not decreasing. ✗
  • Choice B — Decreasing linear: Again, the amount is increasing, not decreasing. ✗
  • Choice C — Increasing exponential: Each year the increase is 3% of the current amount — a constant multiplier of $1.03$. Since the amount being multiplied grows year over year, each year's increase is larger than the last. This is exponential growth, and the amount increases. ✓
  • Choice D — Increasing linear: A linear model would add the same fixed dollar amount each year. But 3% of a growing balance adds more each year, so the growth is not constant. ✗
That matches Choice C — Increasing exponential.
